audit.method: recon + 5 parallel attack-class agents + independent code/runtime verification

findings.title: JWT signing key silently overridable to Helm default "my-secret-key"
findings.attack: pydantic-settings binds env var SECRET_KEY to the Settings.SECRET_KEY field, overriding the AMAZEEAI_JWT_SECRET default. Helm injects SECRET_KEY="my-secret-key" (chart default). Verified: settings.SECRET_KEY resolves to "my-secret-key" even when AMAZEEAI_JWT_SECRET is strong. Attacker forges HS256 JWT {"sub":"<admin-email>"} signed with "my-secret-key" -> full auth bypass as admin.
findings.fix: Read the JWT secret from a single unambiguous source; fail startup if missing or equal to the known default. Remove the my-secret-key chart default. Rotate the secret and invalidate existing tokens.

findings.title: Anonymous privilege escalation via mass-assignment on POST /auth/register (role, team_id)
findings.attack: Anonymous register accepts role and team_id verbatim; role validated only against get_all_roles() which includes admin/key_creator/system_admin/sales. Register with {team_id: VICTIM_TEAM, role: 'admin'} then login -> team admin of victim tenant. With role='system_admin', team_id=null -> chains with C3 to system admin.
findings.fix: Ignore/reject role and team_id on self-registration; require an authenticated invitation flow for team membership.

findings.title: RBAC honors the role column independent of is_admin
findings.attack: _get_effective_role returns raw user.role when is_admin is False. A user with is_admin=False, team_id=None, role='system_admin' passes require_system_admin, reaching DELETE /users, DELETE/merge /teams, GET /teams, admin region assignment, and POST /internal/provision-key (arbitrary LiteLLM/vector-DB provisioning).
findings.fix: Do not derive a system role from user.role unless is_admin is true; enforce invariant role='system_admin' => is_admin=True at write time.

findings.title: key_creator mints LiteLLM tokens owned by cross-team users
findings.attack: Cross-team guard is `user_role == 'admin' and owner.team_id != current_user.team_id`. key_creator role skips it; owner_id never validated. A key_creator in team A sets owner_id to a team B user -> token minted against team B budget and returned in response. Same flaw in create_vector_db.
findings.fix: Apply owner.team_id == current_user.team_id to all team roles; validate owner_id's team in the ownership helper.

findings.title: Team admin bypasses pool-purchase payment gate via mass-assignment on PUT /teams/{id}
findings.attack: update_team guards only is_always_free, then setattr's all TeamUpdate fields including require_purchase_for_requests, budget_type, is_active. Own-team admin sends {require_purchase_for_requests: false} -> requires_pool_purchase_gate flips false -> keys minted unblocked with default budget without purchasing.
findings.verified: source (decorator + setattr loop + schema read directly)
findings.fix: Restrict budget_type, require_purchase_for_requests, is_active to system admins.

findings.title: Unauthenticated, unthrottled /auth/generate-trial-access farms unlimited free AI keys
findings.attack: No auth dependency, no rate limiter in app. Each call creates a unique user (dedupe impossible), grants AI_TRIAL_MAX_BUDGET, provisions real LiteLLM key + vector DB (bypass_delegation=True), returns bearer token. Loop -> unbounded free AI compute + provisioning DoS.
findings.verified: source (endpoint signature + PUBLIC exclusion + no rate-limiter grep)
findings.fix: Per-IP + global rate limit, hard cap on active trials, gate behind email-verification-code flow.

findings.title: Team admin can move arbitrary users into any team
findings.attack: add_user_to_team (get_role_min_team_admin) lacks the team_operation.team_id == current_user.team_id check that create_user/update_user enforce. Any team admin inserts any teamless user into any team.

findings.title: Unbounded budget on non-purchase-gated POOL teams
findings.attack: update_team_budget clamps to purchased total only for purchase-gated POOL teams; a POOL team with require_purchase_for_requests=False hits the unclamped else branch. Own-team admin sets max_budget arbitrarily high with zero payment.
findings.verified: reported by billing agent; consistent with H2 gate logic
findings.fix: Clamp body.max_budget to available/purchased budget for non-gated POOL teams, or restrict to system admins.

findings.title: Region/DB credentials & API tokens stored reversibly at rest
findings.attack: Region litellm_api_key (region master), postgres_admin_password, tenant DB passwords/tokens, system secrets, and API bearer tokens are all plaintext columns. API tokens compared by equality, no hash/expiry. Any DB read (backup, replica, insider, SQLi elsewhere) yields live credentials.
findings.verified: source (model columns + security.py comparison read directly)
findings.fix: Hash API tokens (salted, constant-time, add expiry); encrypt region/DB credentials at rest or use a secrets manager.

findings.title: Passwordless code delivered to un-normalized address, validated against normalized identity
findings.attack: Code emailed to raw +tag address but stored/validated under plus-stripped identity. If attacker receives mail at a +tag variant normalizing to the victim (catch-all, subaddress, auto-provision), submitting the code yields a session for the canonical victim identity / pre-hijack.
findings.fix: Send the code to the same normalized address used for lookup, or bind the code to the exact delivery address.

findings.title: Full OpenAPI schema + Swagger UI exposed unauthenticated
findings.attack: Anonymous GET /openapi.json and /docs enumerate all endpoints (/internal/*, /audit, billing, spend), parameters and schemas -- reconnaissance that directly reveals the role/team_id fields used in C2.
findings.fix: Gate docs/schema behind auth in production.

findings.title: Legacy invoice fan-out multiplies subscription budget across regions
findings.attack: When invoice.paid lacks regionId metadata, full amount_paid is applied to every team region; ledger idempotent only per (invoice, region), so N regions = N x purchased budget.
findings.fix: Split the invoice amount across regions, or refuse cycles without regionId metadata.

findings.title: System-admin-only SSRF via unvalidated region URLs
findings.attack: region.litellm_api_url/postgres_host fetched with no scheme allowlist or internal-IP rejection (169.254.169.254, RFC1918, localhost). Write is system-admin-only, so CSRF-against-admin / defense-in-depth residual only.
findings.verified: reported by injection agent; admin-gated confirmed
findings.fix: Validate region URLs at the schema boundary (scheme allowlist + reject internal/link-local IPs).

findings.title: ENV_SUFFIX defaults to "local", fail-open local-bearer admin bypass
findings.attack: If ENV_SUFFIX is unset it defaults to 'local', re-enabling the local-bearer bypass; with LOCAL_BEARER_USER_EMAIL unset, first admin user is returned -> static bearer = system admin. Requires ENV_SUFFIX absent AND LOCAL_BEARER_TOKEN set; Helm/Lagoon set ENV_SUFFIX explicitly.
findings.fix: Default ENV_SUFFIX to a non-privileged value; gate the bypass on an explicit allow flag.

findings.title: Hardening bundle (host/proxy/config/rate-limit/container)
findings.attack: ALLOWED_HOSTS=['*'] (TrustedHost no-op); X-Forwarded-Proto trusted from any client with forwarded_allow_ips='*'; placeholder secret defaults run silently if env unset; no app-layer rate limiting; verbose region error echoing; no K8s securityContext / Dockerfile USER; verification codes not single-use.
findings.fix: Set real ALLOWED_HOSTS; restrict forwarded_allow_ips to ingress CIDR; assert required secrets present/non-placeholder at startup; enforce edge rate limits; add runAsNonRoot securityContext; make codes single-use.

verified_not_vulnerable: SQL injection (all raw sinks parameterized; dynamic DB provisioning uses UUID identifiers)
Stripe webhook forgery/replay (fail-closed signature verification + idempotency claim row)
JWT algorithm confusion (HS256 pinned, exp verified)
request.state.user spoofing (server-side, re-loaded from DB by PK)
profile-update mass-assignment (UserUpdate has no role/team_id; is_admin blocked for non-admins)
email header injection / markdown XSS (SESv2 structured API, static templates)
